props.text:
# Agent Trigger Demo

Agents can use `trigger` and `replyCondition` to control when and whether they respond.

This demo has **3 pages**, each with one agent using a different reply policy:

1. **Greeter** — `trigger: on_join` — sends a message every time the chat page loads, even if there's already chat history. Try navigating away and back.
2. **Coach** — `trigger: { every: 3 }` — stays silent until every 3rd participant message, then gives feedback. Count your messages!
3. **Facilitator** — `replyCondition: "Reply only if the participant asked a direct question or seems stuck."` — receives every message but uses an LLM check to decide whether to actually respond. Try sending statements vs. questions.

### 1. Greeter — `trigger: on_join`

This agent greets you every time the page loads, even if there's existing chat history.
Try navigating away and coming back — it will greet you again.

### 2. Coach — `trigger: { every: 3 }`

This agent only responds every 3rd message you send.
Send a few messages and count — it will stay silent until message #3.

### 3. Facilitator — `replyCondition`

This agent receives every message but only replies when it thinks you asked a question or seem stuck.
Try sending casual statements vs. direct questions and see the difference.
Thank you for trying the agent trigger demo!

agents.system: You are a friendly greeter. Say a brief welcome message (one sentence).

You are a writing coach. Give brief feedback on the participant's
last few messages. One sentence max.

agents.replyCondition: Reply only if the participant asked a direct question or seems stuck.
agents.system:
You are a helpful facilitator. Answer questions and offer guidance.
Keep responses to 1-2 sentences.
